frame_analyses.description: Production slate: 'PROD. Basics of Laparoscopy' - Scene 1, Take 1, Roll 1, Nov 2020, Directors Dr. Yamini Kale and Dr. Sanjana Sainani
Title slide: 'A Step by Step Approach to Basic Laparoscopy' with presenter credentials
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational framing - not a performance task

frame_analyses.description: Slide showing instructional topics: 'Instruments', 'Patient Position', 'Trocar Entry'
Real laparoscopic tower equipment shown - stacked surgical control console with monitors and instruments
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment overview, not a task performance

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic instruments laid out on green drape: trocars, graspers, scissors, clip appliers
Diagram showing camera port field of view concepts at 0° and 30° angles with Hopkins scope
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational diagram on laparoscope optics and orientation

frame_analyses.description: Labeled diagram of 10mm primary trocar and Veress needle with anatomical components
Close-up view of laparoscopic instruments on green surgical field, showing cannulae and graspers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument detail and handling demonstration setup

frame_analyses.description: Live demonstration of patient positioning on surgical table with positioning apparatus and bolster
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Patient positioning for laparoscopic access - not a task performance

frame_analyses.description: Rendering/model of foam organ surrogate used in laparoscopic training, showing anatomy with appendage
Anatomical diagram showing trocar insertion angle (45°) and relationship to internal organs, with needle and vessel anatomy highlighted
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Safe trocar entry technique and anatomy, educational content

frame_analyses.description: Storz electronic insufflator control panel with green indicator lights visible
Text slide listing methods: 'Veress Needle', 'Direct Trocar Entry', 'Open Technique (Hasson Technique)'

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical diagram showing trocar entry site and relationship to uterus, common iliac artery, peritoneal anatomy
Labeled diagram of laparoscopic trainer showing port positions: 10mm camera port, 5mm working ports, 10mm working port
Title slide reading 'Conclusion' on dark background

confidence_rationale: Video classification is unambiguous. This is a comprehensive educational/instructional video produced for the NOGS (National Obstetric and Gynaecological Surgeons) titled 'Basics of Laparoscopy.' The content includes production credits, presenter credentials, equipment overviews, anatomical diagrams, patient positioning demonstrations, and instructional slides. No actual FLS task performance (ligating loop deployment around a foam appendage) is shown. The video is narrated educational content with overlays, diagrams, equipment demonstrations, and teaching framework—classic hallmarks of instructional content. No performance scoring is applicable.
cannot_determine: Task performance - no endoloop task execution is shown in this video

demonstrated_techniques: Trocar insertion angles and anatomy (45° entry angle shown)
Camera port field-of-view orientation (0° vs 30° scopes)
Safe anatomical landmarks for trocar entry (avoiding iliac vessels)
Patient positioning and bolster setup for supine laparoscopy
FLS trainer port configuration (10mm camera, 5mm and 10mm working ports)
verbal_cues: Title: 'A Step by Step Approach to Basic Laparoscopy'
Methods listed: Veress Needle, Direct Trocar Entry, Open Technique (Hasson Technique)
Equipment components identified: Hopkins scope, Veress needle, 10mm primary trocar, graspers, scissors, clip appliers
Anatomical structures labeled: Uterus, common iliac artery, peritoneal layers
common_errors_addressed: Proper trocar entry angle (45° angle shown to avoid vascular injury)
Recognition of anatomical landmarks to prevent major vessel puncture
Correct patient positioning to optimize laparoscopic access
Safe insufflation technique (Storz insufflator shown with proper settings)
teaching_content.note: This is an expert-level educational production created to teach fundamentals of laparoscopy to surgical trainees. Dr. Yamini Kale (MBBS, DNB, FMAS) and Dr. Sanjana Sainani (MBBS, MS, FMAS) are presenting expert-curated content. The video is designed for foundational laparoscopic knowledge acquisition, not task performance evaluation.

video_summary: This is a professional instructional video produced in November 2020 for the NOGS teaching 'Basics of Laparoscopy' with expert narration, anatomical diagrams, equipment demonstrations, and training setup. No FLS endoloop task performance is present. Scoring is not applicable; teaching content has been extracted.
recommendation: Do not score. This is instructional/educational content, not a trainee performance video. Teaching content extraction complete.
